zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Gleason Jr.於1547年出生在英國諾薩普頓史爾素爾格拉福時，他的父親Robert C. Leeson19歲，母親Susan Stotesbury20歲。他於1561年1月30日與Jane Lowe在英國諾薩普頓史爾格亞特 霍頓結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和3個女兒的父母。他去世於1614年9月30日在他的家鄉享年67歲，埋葬在英國諾薩普頓史爾素爾格拉福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas Gleason Jr.于1547年出生在英国諾薩普頓史爾素爾格拉福时，他的父亲Robert C. Leeson19岁，母亲Susan Stotesbury20岁。他于1561年1月30日与Jane Lowe在英国諾薩普頓史爾格亞特 霍頓结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和3个女儿的父母。他去世于1614年9月30日在他的家乡享年67岁，埋葬在英国諾薩普頓史爾素爾格拉福。

nameMeanings.name: Gleason
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Irish (Waterford): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Glasáin, from a diminutive of glas ‘green, blue, gray’.

variants.name: Glasheen
meanings: Irish (Munster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Glaisín,based on a diminutive of glas ‘green, blue, gray’. Compare Gleason .

variants.name: Glisan
meanings: Possibly an altered form of Irish   Gleason  . This surname is concentrated in IL and PA.

variants.name: Gleeson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Donal, Siobhan, Brendan, Conor.

nameMeanings.name: Jr.
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Sang, Yong, Kyung, Wei, Young, Yuhe, Chen, Dong, Fang, Guoping, Hongbin.  Korean Chang, Dae, Min, Bok Nam, Byung, Do Young, Dong Sik, Hak, Jong Won, Jung Hwan, Jung Ok, Kwang Sun.

Mandarin form of the surname 紀, meaning ‘record’ or ‘era’ in Chinese: (i) from Ji (紀), the name of an ancient state (located in Shouguang in Shandong province) granted to descendants of the legendary Yan Di, the Yan Emperor. After the state was annexed by the state of Qi in 550  BC , the name of the state was adopted as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of another Chinese surname, Shu (舒), during the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ).

variants.name: Kit
meanings: Ukrainian and Rusyn: nickname from Ukrainian and Rusyn kit ‘cat’. As a Rusyn name it is also found in Poland.

Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 揭, meaning ‘raise, hoist’ in ancient Chinese: shortened form of the placename Jie Yang (揭陽) (located in present-day eastern Guangdong province), possibly borne by descendants of the governor of Jieyang originally called Shi Ding, also known as Jie Yang Ling (揭陽令) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC  - 25  AD ).
Chinese: Teochew, Hokkien, or Taiwanese form of the surname 吉, see   Ji   7.

variants.name: Kei
meanings: Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname of 紀, see   Ji   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 祁, see   Qi   2.
Japanese: written 荊 ‘briar’; the character can also be pronounced   Ibara  . Another version is written 慶 ‘rejoicing’. Both are rare in Japan.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The Royal Exchange is Organized
historicEvents.description: The Royal Exchange was organized in January 1571 by Sir Thomas Gresham. Gresham is known as the father of English banking.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Gleason Jr. was born in 1547, in Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, England, his father, Robert C. Leeson, was 19 and his mother, Susan Stotesbury, was 20. He married Jane Lowe on 30 January 1561, in Great Houghton, Northamptonshire, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 3 daughters. He died on 30 September 1614, in his hometown, at the age of 67, and was buried in Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Gleason Jr. est né en 1547, à Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, Angleterre, son père, Robert C. Leeson, avait 19 ans et sa mère, Susan Stotesbury, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Jane Lowe le 30 janvier 1561, à Great Houghton, Northamptonshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 3 filles. Il est décédé le 30 septembre 1614, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas Gleason Jr. 1547 in Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Robert C. Leeson 19 und seine Mutter Susan Stotesbury 20. Er hat am 30. Januar 1561 in Great Houghton, Northamptonshire, England Jane Lowe geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb am 30. September 1614 im Alter von 67 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Gleason Jr. è nato il nel 1547 nel Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, England, il suo padre, Robert C. Leeson, aveva 19 anni e il suo madre, Susan Stotesbury, aveva 20 anni. Lui sposato Jane Lowe il 30 gennaio 1561, nel Great Houghton, Northamptonshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 3 figlie. Morì il 30 settembre 1614, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Gleason Jr.が1547年にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアスルグラブで生まれた時，父Robert C. Leesonは19歳，母Susan Stotesburyは20歳であった。1561年1月30日にイギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアグレアト ホーウトンでJane Loweと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と3人の娘の親であった。彼は1614年9月30日に故郷で67歳で死去し，イギリスノーサンプトンシュアイアスルグラブにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Gleason Jr.(이)가 1547년 영국 노르섬프토실르 설그레이브에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Robert C. Leeson(은)는 19세였고, 그의 어머니 Susan Stotesbury(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1561년 1월 30일 영국 노르섬프토실르 그리트 허그h턴에서 Jane Lowe(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1614년 9월 30일 그의 고향에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 노르섬프토실르 설그레이브에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas Gleason Jr. nasceu em 1547, em Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert C. Leeson, tinha 19 anos e sua mãe, Susan Stotesbury, tinha 20 anos. Ele casou-se com Jane Lowe em 30 de janeiro de 1561, em Great Houghton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 3 filhas. Ele faleceu em 30 de setembro de 1614, em sua cidade natal, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas Gleason Jr. родился в 1547 г. в местности сулграв, нортамптоншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Robert C. Leeson было 19 лет, а матери, которую звали Susan Stotesbury, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Jane Lowe 30 января 1561 г. в местности грит хоугтон, нортамптоншир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 3 дочери. Он умер 30 сентября 1614 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности сулграв, нортамптоншир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas Gleason Jr. nació en 1547, en Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert C. Leeson, tenía 19 años y su mamá, Susan Stotesbury, tenía 20 años. Se casó con Jane Lowe el 30 de enero de 1561, en Great Houghton, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 3 hijas. Murió el 30 de septiembre de 1614, en su pueblo, a la edad de 67 años, y fue sepultado en Sulgrave, Northamptonshire, Inglaterra.
